title: Chilli Paneer

subs.name: firm tofu pressed and dried, for a vegan version
subs.notes: mark dairy-free; use soy-free sauce too

ingredients.name: cornflour
visual_checks: 3 tbsp for batter, 1 tsp for sauce slurry
ingredients.notes: 3 tbsp for batter, 1 tsp for sauce slurry

subs.name: rice flour for a gluten-free batter

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated
oil
visual_checks: 3 tbsp for shallow-frying paneer plus 1 tbsp for sauce
ingredients.notes: 3 tbsp for shallow-frying paneer plus 1 tbsp for sauce

steps.text: In a bowl mix 3 tbsp cornflour, maida, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, black pepper and salt. Add just enough water — about 3 tbsp — to make a thick batter. Coat the paneer cubes in this batter, pressing gently so the batter adheres.

Heat 3 tbsp oil in a wide kadai or frying pan on medium-high. Place battered paneer cubes in a single layer and shallow-fry for 2 minutes per side until golden and slightly crisp on all faces. Remove and keep warm — do not drain on paper towel or the steam will soften the crust.
Mix 1 tsp cornflour with 2 tbsp cold water for a slurry. Combine soy sauce, vinegar, ketchup, schezwan sauce and sugar in a small bowl.

In the same kadai, add 1 tbsp oil on high heat. Add spring onion whites and green chilli and stir for 20 seconds. Add remaining ginger-garlic paste, onion layers and capsicum and stir-fry for 2 minutes on the highest flame until slightly charred at the edges.
Pour in the sauce blend and bring to a rapid bubble. Stir in the cornflour slurry and cook 30 seconds until the sauce thickens into a glossy, clingy coat.

Add the fried paneer and toss gently so every cube is coated without crumbling. Check salt and adjust. Scatter spring onion greens on top and serve at once — as a starter, or alongside fried rice or noodles.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: paneer → firm tofu pressed and dried, for a vegan version (mark dairy-free; use soy-free sauce too); maida → rice flour for a gluten-free batter; green-chilli-sauce → 1 tbsp sriracha or sambal oelek; soy-sauce → coconut aminos
